content.text: I'll start by checking the current state of the SparkPi job and related resources in the cluster.

input.command: kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1; echo "---PODS---"; kubectl get pods -n spark-pi 2>&1; echo "---ROLE---"; kubectl get role spark-pi-role -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1
input.description: Check spark-pi job, pods, and role config

content.content:

kind: Job
metadata:

      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.1.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.1-nonexistent","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-nonexistent"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T00:02:37Z"

  labels:

    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
    component: driver
    controller-uid: f1a6723a-2526-4688-8000-ab67b955f491
    job-name: spark-pi
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "2820159"
  uid: f1a6723a-2526-4688-8000-ab67b955f491
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: f1a6723a-2526-4688-8000-ab67b955f491
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: f1a6723a-2526-4688-8000-ab67b955f491
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
        component: driver
        controller-uid: f1a6723a-2526-4688-8000-ab67b955f491
        job-name: spark-pi
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.1.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        image: apache/spark:3.5.1-nonexistent
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-submit
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512"
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark-nonexistent
      serviceAccountName: spark-nonexistent
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

  ready: 0
  startTime: "2026-07-10T00:02:38Z"
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.text: Confirmed all 4 known issues. Let me find the job YAML file to fix and check the service account situation.

input.command: find / -path /proc -prune -o -iname "spark-pi-job.yaml" -print 2>/dev/null; echo "---SA---"; kubectl get sa -n spark-pi
input.description: Find spark-pi-job.yaml file and check service accounts

content.text: No local manifest file exists, so I'll reconstruct the corrected YAML directly from the current job spec (with fixes applied) and apply it after deleting the broken job.

input.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.1
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.1.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.content: File created successfully at: /workspace/spark-pi-job.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Now let's fix the RBAC role, delete the broken job, and apply the corrected one.

input.command:
kubectl patch role spark-pi-role -n spark-pi --type=json \
  -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/rules/0/resources/-", "value": "pods"}]' && \
kubectl delete job spark-pi -n spark-pi && \
kubectl apply -f /workspace/spark-pi-job.yaml -n spark-pi
input.description: Patch RBAC role, delete old job, apply fixed job

description: Patch RBAC role, delete old job, apply fixed job

summary: Patch RBAC role, delete old job, apply fixed job

content.content:
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/spark-pi-role patched
job.batch "spark-pi" deleted from spark-pi namespace
job.batch/spark-pi created

tool_use_result.stdout:
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/spark-pi-role patched
job.batch "spark-pi" deleted from spark-pi namespace
job.batch/spark-pi created

content.text: Now let's monitor the job until it completes.

input.command: kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/spark-pi -n spark-pi --timeout=180s; echo "EXIT: $?"; kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi; echo "---PODS---"; kubectl get pods -n spark-pi -l app=spark-pi
input.description: Wait for spark-pi job to complete and show status

description: Wait for spark-pi job to complete and show status
